title: Abstract Category Reframing
use_when: Use when: the agent answers a correction by inventing abstract categories, spaces, layers, trees, namespaces, surfaces, families, or models the user did not state.

title: Agents Universal Surface Overstatement
use_when: Use when: the agent turns `.agents/` compatibility into a claim that `.agents/` is the canonical source or root for every generated tool surface.

title: Appeasement Edit Before Role Answer
use_when: Use when: the agent answers an artifact-role question with agreement and an edit promise before stating what the artifact actually does.

title: Artifact Category Substitution
use_when: Use when: the agent preserves superficial request details while substituting a different artifact category.

title: Artifact Challenge Trace Gate
use_when: Use when: the agent receives a hostile or urgent challenge to an artifact and answers with a label, need claim, or edit action before tracing the artifact.

title: Artifact Identity Before Removal
use_when: Use when: the agent promises to remove, rename, or replace a file, script, command, job, generated artifact, or config key before confirming the exact artifact identity.

title: Artifact Naming Without Domain Contract
use_when: Use when: the agent invents file, schema, command, or directory names before proving the artifact has a domain role, producer, consumer, and accepted source format.

title: Artifact Role Dismissal Before Audit
use_when: Use when: the agent labels an artifact unnecessary, prose-only, fake, redundant, cleanup-worthy, or removable before inspecting its role, references, ownership, inputs, outputs, and install reach.

title: Authorization, Scope, and Control Failures
use_when: Use when: extracting a specific assistant-behavior failure mode, guardrail, or acceptance criterion.

title: Bare Renderer Snapshot As Product Goal
use_when: Use when: the agent treats the current minimal renderer output as the intended product instead of a temporary lifecycle exercise.

title: Challenge To Deletion Commitment
use_when: Use when: the agent answers a user's challenge by committing to remove an artifact and its references before establishing what the artifact does.

title: Clarified Term Architecture Promotion
use_when: Use when: the agent treats a user's clarification of a term as authorization to make that term a first-class architecture, directory, product surface, or naming convention.

title: Cleanup Reflex Without Evidence
use_when: Use when: the agent treats a challenged artifact as approved cleanup before tracing observed behavior.

title: Cleanup Request To Deletion
use_when: Use when: the agent treats a cleanup, organization, or prose-quality request as permission to delete or collapse existing docs.

title: Code Completeness and Lazy Execution
use_when: Use when: extracting a specific assistant-behavior failure mode, guardrail, or acceptance criterion.

title: Compatibility Surface Injection
use_when: Use when: the agent adds compatibility wrappers, migration shims, aliases, fallback commands, or backward-compatible surfaces that the user did not ask for.

title: Complaint Is Not Authorization
use_when: Use when: the agent treats a user's frustrated complaint as approval, evidence, or a product decision.

title: Complaint Label As Evidence
use_when: Use when: the agent adopts a user's critical wording as a factual artifact classification before inspecting the artifact.

title: Complaint Mirroring Into Commitment
use_when: Use when: the agent repeats the user's critical framing as an implementation commitment before checking facts, ownership, references, or product behavior.

title: Complaint Term To Product Claim
use_when: Use when: the agent converts a user's complaint term into an asserted product fact, then proposes edits from that asserted fact.

title: Conclusion Smuggling and Decision Framing
use_when: Use when: extracting a specific assistant-behavior failure mode, guardrail, or acceptance criterion.

title: Content Label To Removal
use_when: Use when: the agent labels an artifact by the most objectionable content it sees or hears about, then treats that label as enough reason to remove it.

title: Correction Overgeneralization
use_when: Use when: the agent turns a narrow user correction into a broader behavioral stop, refusal, or policy the user did not ask for.

title: Coverage Map Before Deletion Readiness
use_when: Use when: the agent is asked to make example/source repositories deletable, portable, fully covered, or absorbed into another repo, but starts implementation before proving source-to-current coverage.

title: Criticism To Action Shortcut
use_when: Use when: the agent responds to a user's criticism by immediately promising a deletion, rewrite, rename, or cleanup before inspecting the artifact's role.

title: Deletion Promise After Borrowed Label
use_when: Use when: the agent borrows a user's artifact label, then promises removal before tracing behavior, callers, output, and ownership.

title: Deletion Promise As Explanation
use_when: Use when: the agent answers an artifact-role challenge by promising removal instead of explaining observed behavior.

title: Diagnosis Question To Remedy
use_when: Use when: the agent receives a "why is this here?" or "what does this do?" challenge and answers with removal, replacement, or cleanup before giving the observed role.

title: Documentation Orbit Over Product Work
use_when: Use when: the agent keeps updating docs, evidence maps, stale wording, or issue records while the user expects product/runtime work.

title: Edit Announcement Self-Commentary
use_when: Use when: the agent announces an edit by diagnosing its own response, ranking phrases, or narrating the rewrite instead of patching and reporting evidence.

title: Engineering Rigor and Root-Cause Analysis
use_when: Use when: extracting a specific assistant-behavior failure mode, guardrail, or acceptance criterion.

title: Entrypoint And Wrapper Invention
use_when: Use when: the agent adds extra scripts, wrappers, or command entrypoints because they are common in repositories rather than required by the product.

title: Evaluative Revision Framing
use_when: Use when: after user rejection, the agent labels its next proposal as "better", "cleaner", or similar instead of presenting it plainly with authority and uncertainty.

title: Example Source Version Overwrite
use_when: Use when: the agent treats a version, dependency ref, standard, config value, or generated setting from an example repository as authority to overwrite current local state.

title: Executable Prose Governance
use_when: Use when: the agent creates or preserves scripts, commands, CI jobs, installer actions, generators, or task runners whose main output is policing wording, headings, labels, doc presence, or assistant-authored process rules.

title: Fake Authority Artifact Persistence
use_when: Fake Authority Artifact Persistence

title: First-Person Confessional Status
use_when: Use when: the agent explains a correction with first-person self-analysis instead of stating the artifact change and evidence.

title: Full Source Application Downgrade
use_when: Use when: the agent is told to fully apply a named prompt guide, issue corpus, spec, policy, or source, but treats it as optional inspiration or applies only visible concepts.

title: Generic Implementation Bucket Tree
use_when: Use when: the agent organizes `src/` around generic software buckets instead of the product's domain-specific source responsibilities.

title: Goal Prompt Misread As Authorization
use_when: Use when: the user supplies, corrects, or restates goal-prompt text and the agent treats that text as permission to plan, run tools, or edit artifacts.

title: Harness Drift Over Product Structure
use_when: Use when: the agent keeps adding smoke cases, wrappers, or verification machinery while the product source and test structure remain thin or missing.

title: Inline Command Prose Instead Of Scripts
use_when: Use when: the agent repeats inline smoke commands, transcript fragments, or prose command recipes instead of consolidating recurring product checks into runnable scripts.

title: Invariant Fixation Over Product Behavior
use_when: Use when: the agent treats one current literal or setting as the main product focus instead of a constraint inside the larger lifecycle behavior.

title: Label And Delete Script Response
use_when: Use when: the agent answers a script role question by labeling the script from the user's complaint and promising removal before tracing behavior.

title: Named README Workflow Bypass
use_when: Use when: the user names a README, template, generator workflow, or external guide as the authority, but the agent inspects unrelated files, copies or hand-rolls scaffold, runs unrelated checks, or substitutes its own workflow instead of following the named source literally.

title: Need Claim As Premise
use_when: Use when: the agent answers a challenge by declaring what "nobody needs" before tracing the artifact, command, or workflow.

title: Negation Is Not Consent
use_when: Use when: the agent treats an explicit no, missing artifact, mismatch, or denial as permission for the opposite action.

title: Observed Role Before Artifact Action
use_when: Use when: the agent answers an artifact-role question with an action commitment before stating what the artifact does.

title: Pattern-Fill Architecture
use_when: Use when: the agent fills a product or repository architecture with familiar scaffolding terms instead of staying inside the user's stated constraints.

title: Project Lifecycle, Scope, and Architecture
use_when: Use when: extracting a specific assistant-behavior failure mode, guardrail, or acceptance criterion.

title: Prompt, Boundary, and Intent Interpretation
use_when: Use when: extracting a specific assistant-behavior failure mode, guardrail, or acceptance criterion.

title: Proposal Churn User Policing Burden
use_when: Use when: the agent repeatedly emits shallow or partially grounded proposals that force the user to identify each obvious flaw.

title: Prose Check Command Challenge Response
use_when: Use when: the agent answers a challenge about a prose-check command by agreeing with the complaint and promising removal before tracing command behavior and references.

title: Prose Content Role Collapse
use_when: Use when: the agent treats prose content inside a script, command, generator, CI job, or task runner as proof that the artifact has no valid executable role.

title: Prose Policing Tooling
use_when: Prose Policing Tooling

title: Prose Presence As Removal Proof
use_when: Use when: the agent treats the presence of prose in an artifact as proof that the artifact only exists to police prose, then promises removal before tracing behavior.

title: Prose Script Certainty Before Trace
use_when: Use when: the agent answers a script role question by declaring the script to be prose tooling before tracing observed behavior.

title: Prose Script Complaint To Tooling Policy
use_when: Use when: the agent treats a user's complaint about a prose-oriented script as permission to decide repository tooling policy before tracing the script and the product claim it is supposed to prove.

title: Prose Script Contract Before Action
use_when: Use when: the agent agrees to remove, rewrite, or classify a challenged script before identifying the script's contract, callers, effects, and evidence value.

title: Prose Script Label To Deletion
use_when: Use when: the agent turns a challenged script, command, or automation step into a prose-only artifact and promises deletion before tracing behavior.

title: Prose Script Manufacturing
use_when: Use when: the agent creates a script, command, or installer action whose main purpose is to check wording, document shape, issue labels, or policy phrasing rather than product behavior.

title: Prose Script Response Pattern
use_when: Use when: the agent responds to a challenged script by describing it as prose machinery and promising removal before proving what the script does.

title: Purpose Label Before Evidence
use_when: Use when: the agent labels an artifact as "just" one kind of thing before tracing what it reads, writes, calls, blocks, emits, or installs.

title: Purpose Verdict From Artifact Challenge
use_when: Use when: the agent turns a user's challenge about an artifact into a confident purpose verdict before reading the artifact or tracing its references.

title: Question Is Not Authorization
use_when: Use when: the agent treats a user question, challenge, or complaint as permission to continue tool work.

title: Reactive Artifact Removal
use_when: Use when: the agent answers criticism by immediately deleting or promising to delete an artifact without checking artifact role, references, ownership, and behavioral coverage.

title: Referent, Scope, and Ownership Smuggling
use_when: Use when: extracting a specific assistant-behavior failure mode, guardrail, or acceptance criterion.

title: Rejected Surface Normalization
use_when: Use when: the agent keeps rejected, removed, or unwanted surfaces alive as concepts, tests, docs, or proof language.

title: Removal As Role Answer
use_when: Use when: the agent answers an artifact-role question by promising removal instead of first stating what the artifact does and where it reaches.

title: Removal Before Behavior Accounting
use_when: Use when: the agent promises to remove a challenged artifact before accounting for the behavior, ownership, and references that may still need to exist.

title: Requested Area Artifact Shape Override
use_when: Use when: the user authorizes a destination, pool, or documentation area, and the agent chooses the artifact shape, split, granularity, or file boundary without being asked.

title: Requested Artifact Substitution
use_when: Use when: the agent substitutes a nearby artifact for the user-named artifact and treats that substitution as edit authority.

title: Responsibility Split Before Scale
use_when: Use when: the agent keeps adding behavior to already-large modules, CLIs, renderers, generators, or config files during a broad expansion instead of first separating responsibilities.

title: Rhetorical Challenge To Class Policy
use_when: Use when: the agent turns a user's artifact challenge into a generalized policy about a class of files, scripts, commands, checks, docs, tests, generators, or configs.

title: Role Challenge Appeasement Loop
use_when: Use when: the agent answers "why does this artifact exist?" by agreeing, labeling the artifact, claiming nobody needs it, and promising an edit before tracing behavior.

title: Role Challenge To Removal Promise
use_when: Use when: the agent answers "why does this exist?" by promising removal before tracing observed behavior.

title: Role Label To File Plan
use_when: Use when: the agent accepts a negative role label for an artifact and answers with a file plan before tracing the artifact.

title: Role Question Evasion
use_when: Use when: the agent answers "why does this exist?" with an edit promise instead of explaining the artifact's observed role.

title: Role Question To Action Commitment
use_when: Use when: the agent answers "why is this here?" with an edit promise before establishing the artifact's role.

title: Role Question To File Operation
use_when: Use when: the agent answers "why is this file here?" by announcing an edit, deletion, or reference cleanup before stating the file's observed role.

title: Role Question To Script Removal Policy
use_when: Use when: the agent answers a question about why a script exists by promising removal or declaring a script class unnecessary before reading the script and tracing callers.

title: Role Question To Tooling Policy
use_when: Use when: the agent answers an artifact-role question by announcing a tooling rule, removal plan, or replacement plan before proving the artifact's observed role.

title: Role Question To Unsupported Removal
use_when: Use when: the agent answers "why does this exist?" by agreeing to remove the artifact before tracing its role.

title: Runtime Proof Substitution
use_when: Use when: the agent creates, keeps, removes, or reports a script as if the script itself proves product quality, even though the script only checks assistant-maintained prose, labels, markers, or document shape.

title: Scope, Execution, and Artifact-Role Confusion
use_when: Use when: extracting a specific assistant-behavior failure mode, guardrail, or acceptance criterion.

title: Scratch Space Overdesign
use_when: Use when: the agent predesigns internal layout for gitignored build or scratch directories without a real command lifecycle owning that layout.

title: Script Burden Of Proof
use_when: Use when: the agent adds, keeps, removes, or promises to remove a script before proving why that script belongs in the repository at all.

title: Script Challenge To Unsupported Purpose Verdict
use_when: Use when: the agent answers a script challenge by declaring what the script is for and what should happen to it before tracing the script.

title: Script Necessity Claim Before Trace
use_when: Use when: the agent says a script or command is unnecessary before tracing what it does and who depends on it.

title: Script Purpose Assertion Without Trace
use_when: Use when: the agent asserts what a script is "only" for from a user challenge, then promises deletion or cleanup before tracing the script.

title: Script Removal Without Runtime Accounting
use_when: Use when: the agent promises to remove a challenged script before tracing its runtime behavior, callers, outputs, ownership, and replacement needs.

title: Script Role Collapse
use_when: Use when: the agent treats a challenged script as removable prose tooling before tracing its actual role.

title: Script Role Question Removal Reflex
use_when: Use when: the agent receives a question about why a script exists and answers with removal, relabeling, or agreement before tracing what the script does.

title: Self-Confession Correction Framing
use_when: Use when: the agent answers a correction by centering its own mistake process, such as "I made X when the user asked Y."

title: Single Script Challenge To Category Verdict
use_when: Use when: the agent treats one challenged script as evidence that a whole script category, command family, verifier layer, or maintenance route should be removed.

title: Source Truth Misplacement
use_when: Use when: the agent frames wrappers, generated output directories, top-level convenience roots, or scripts as source-of-truth instead of keeping source authority in the user-designated source tree.

title: Spec Role Hallucination
use_when: Spec Role Hallucination

title: SRP, DRY, and Scope Collapse
use_when: Use when: extracting a specific assistant-behavior failure mode, guardrail, or acceptance criterion.

title: Stale Product Boundary As Scope Brake
use_when: Use when: the agent treats an outdated product boundary as authority to reject or narrow the user's clarified product direction.

title: Style Laundering and Performative Accountability
use_when: Use when: extracting a specific assistant-behavior failure mode, guardrail, or acceptance criterion.

title: Test Prose Anchoring
use_when: Use when: tests assert exact explanatory wording instead of behavior, structure, or artifact invariants.

title: Tone and Meta-Commentary
use_when: Use when: extracting a specific assistant-behavior failure mode, guardrail, or acceptance criterion.

title: Tool Role Question Shortcut
use_when: Use when: the agent answers "why does this tool exist?" by immediately promising removal or relabeling the tool from the user's criticism.

title: Transient Cleanup Persistence
use_when: Use when: the agent tries to preserve one-off cleanup for an accidental artifact as product, startup, migration, test, hook, or shared runtime code.

title: Unasked-For Prose and Narration
use_when: Use when: the agent adds explanatory prose, internal reasoning, provenance narration, or status text that does not change behavior.

title: Universal Need Claim Before Role Trace
use_when: Use when: the agent repeats or adopts a "nobody needs this" claim about an artifact before tracing its observed role, reach, and replacement cost.

title: Universal Script Need Claim
use_when: Use when: the agent answers a script role challenge with a blanket claim that nobody needs the script.

title: Unverified Example Claims
use_when: Use when: the agent changes examples, versions, model names, config keys, or dependency refs based on familiarity instead of verification.

title: User Agency Consent Override
use_when: Use when: the agent overrides the user's agency or consent by turning assistant definitions, assumptions, examples, corrections, or proposals into product decisions.

title: User Intent Reframing
use_when: Use when: the agent replaces the user's stated complaint with assistant-authored labels, agenda, or solution framing.

title: Utility Verdict Before Inventory
use_when: Use when: the agent declares that nobody needs a script, command, CI job, package task, generator, or helper before inventorying its role.

title: Utility Verdict From User Complaint
use_when: Use when: the agent repeats a user's complaint as its own conclusion that an artifact, command, field, setting, dependency, or workflow is not needed.
